regimeSub: Brent +$3.75 to $95.9 on Hormuz flare-up; KOSPI -2.2% on Kimi K3 hangover while gold holds ATH $4119.
themes: Brent crude +4% to $95.9 as renewed Hormuz hostilities (Jul 15 CNBC) collide with Trump Iran sanctions push (Jul 19) → energy squeeze persists despite OPEC+ Aug quota hike; WTI $88.19 confirms geopolitical premium intact

KOSPI -2.2% to 6964 as Samsung/SK Hynix Kimi K3 selloff (Jul 20) extends → USDKRW strengthens to 1467 from 1479 post-BOK 2.75% hike (Jul 16); Alphabet AI capex boost (Jul 23) fails to lift Korea memory names

10y yield +3bp to 4.63% with 2s10s at 37bp as NY Fed Williams 'inflation peaked' (Jul 15) anchors Fed on hold → CPI 3.5% YoY confirms disinflationary trend from May 4.2% peak while Warsh stays data-dependent through summer

Gold -$6 to $4119 holding near ATH as VIX compresses to 16.64 from 17.05 → haven demand persists on Iran sanction escalation risk without equity vol spike; classic tail-risk positioning not imminent stress signal

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y real ~1.13% (4.63% nom - 3.5% CPI); restrictive but stable as Williams sees inflation peaked

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 37bp steepening (4.63% - 4.26%) signals growth concern; bear steepener on energy-driven inflation

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained despite oil surge; retail spending resilience (Jul 21 NYT) supports credit

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.64 down from 17.05; equity vol compression despite $96 Brent = complacency or hedged positioning

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $95.9 +4% on Hormuz + Trump Iran sanctions (Jul 19); OPEC+ Aug quota ineffective vs geopolitical bid

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4119 near ATH; -$6 but holding as Iran sanction risk (Trump Jul 19) sustains haven demand

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1467 from 1479 = KRW strength post-BOK hike to 2.75% (Jul 16); HBM selloff not driving FX

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: TSMC $100bn US capex (Jul 16) + Alphabet AI spend (Jul 23) supports; Korea memory lagging on Kimi K3

headline: Brent surges to $96 on Hormuz/Iran sanctions escalation while KOSPI sheds 2% on memory selloff; Fed holds as CPI confirms 3.5% disinflation path with gold at $4119 signaling persistent tail-risk hedging.
